data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Fills Key CME Gap Below $88K as BTC Slips, Traders Remain Defensive
data.description: Bitcoin briefly dipped below $88,000 on Wednesday, filling a key Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) futures gap formed at the start of the year — a move closely watched by traders as a potential short-term turning point.BTC rebounded modestly toward the $90,000 level after touching intraday lows near $87,800, but analysts cautioned that the bounce lacks conviction, with broader market sentiment still tilted toward capital preservation.BTC erases January gains after CME gap fillAccording to TradingView data, Bitcoin’s decline marked its lowest price since the beginning of January, wiping out more than $10,000 in gains from earlier monthly highs.The move completed a long-watched CME futures gap from the yearly open — a technical level that often acts as a price “magnet,” drawing spot markets back before any sustained trend resumes.With the sub-$88,000 gap now closed, traders note that three additional CME gaps remain above current price levels, near:$97,800$113,400$116,900While some analysts argue the completed gap removes near-term downside pressure, others remain skeptical about Bitcoin’s ability to regain upside momentum.Traders divided as technical structure weakensMarket reaction following the gap fill has been mixed.Some traders suggested the move was necessary to reset positioning before any potential rebound. Others highlighted deteriorating structure on higher timeframes, pointing to a failed breakout attempt and renewed pressure along descending trendlines.One commonly cited concern is the formation of a lower low following a recent higher high — a pattern often associated with weakening trend continuation.As one trader summarized, Bitcoin is now “back to square one for the year,” with price once again testing key technical support rather than extending higher.Macro pressure keeps crypto in defensive modeBeyond technical factors, macroeconomic conditions continue to weigh heavily on crypto markets.In its latest Asia market update, trading firm QCP Capital said Bitcoin is behaving increasingly like a high-beta macro asset, reacting sharply to:Interest rate expectationsGeopolitical riskCross-asset volatility“Until clearer policy signals emerge, crypto is likely to remain reactive rather than directional,” QCP noted, adding that markets are currently prioritizing capital preservation over conviction.Gold hits new records as Bitcoin lagsWhile Bitcoin struggles to regain traction, traditional safe-haven assets continue to outperform.Gold surged to a fresh all-time high near $4,888 per ounce, extending its rally amid rising geopolitical tension and trade uncertainty. The divergence has reinforced a short-term risk-off narrative, with capital favoring metals over digital assets.Market observers say the contrast underscores Bitcoin’s current sensitivity to macro stress, rather than its long-term store-of-value narrative.What traders are watching nextWith the January CME gap now filled, attention shifts to whether Bitcoin can:Hold above the $88,000–$90,000 zoneReclaim lost trendline supportAttract renewed spot demandUntil then, analysts say the market remains vulnerable to volatility spikes, with upside dependent on clearer policy direction or a return of institutional risk appetite.For now, Bitcoin remains range-bound — and cautious sentiment continues to dominate the tape, according to Cointelegraph.

data.title: Institutional Influence Alters Cryptocurrency Market Dynamics
data.description:
Garrett Jin, representative of '1011 Insider Whale,' shared insights on X platform regarding Wintermute's perspective on the cryptocurrency market. According to Odaily, Wintermute's view has been interpreted as signaling the end of traditional bull market cycles in cryptocurrency. However, Jin argues that institutions have not ended the bull market but have transformed it. As institutional asset management scales up, asset prices tend to rise while volatility decreases. Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) are transitioning from speculative mechanisms to allocation mechanisms.

Wintermute's actual stance is that with deeper institutional involvement, leading cryptocurrencies like BTC and ETH are evolving from speculative tools to configurable assets. The current market changes do not signify the end of a bull market but rather a shift from a 'retail-driven, high-volatility, meme-dominated' phase to a 'allocation-driven, institutionally positioned, institutional-grade volatility' mechanism.

data.title: Crypto News: Ripple President Predicts Crypto Adoption Across 50% of Fortune 500 by 2026
data.description: Ripple president Monica Long says blockchain is rapidly becoming the core infrastructure of global finance, predicting that half of Fortune 500 companies will actively use digital assets or blockchain-based financial instruments by the end of 2026.In a blog post published Monday, Long said the crypto industry has spent recent years building the technical and regulatory foundation needed to support large-scale institutional adoption — a process she believes is now reaching an inflection point.“By the end of 2026, balance sheets will hold over $1 trillion in digital assets,” Long wrote, adding that roughly 250 Fortune 500 companies are expected to formalize digital asset strategies.Blockchain becoming the operating layer of financeAccording to Long, corporate adoption will extend far beyond passive crypto exposure.She expects companies to actively engage in:Tokenized real-world assetsDigital asset treasuriesStablecoin settlement systemsOnchain Treasury billsProgrammable financial instruments“Blockchain is becoming the operating layer of modern finance,” she said.Long cited a Coinbase survey from mid-2025 showing that six out of ten Fortune 500 executives said their companies were already exploring blockchain initiatives — a figure she expects to accelerate sharply in 2026.Corporate Bitcoin treasuries continue to expandWhile only a limited number of Fortune 500 firms currently hold Bitcoin, Long pointed to growing momentum among public companies.GameStop became one of the latest entrants after purchasing 4,710 BTC in May 2025, joining firms such as Tesla and Block Inc.She also highlighted the rapid rise of digital asset treasury (DAT) companies, noting that:The number has grown from just four in 2020 to more than 200 todayNearly 100 DAT firms were launched in 2025 aloneThe expansion reflects a broader shift toward crypto-native balance sheet management among both traditional and emerging companies.Stablecoins to become backbone of global settlementLong made one of her strongest forecasts around stablecoins, predicting they will soon become foundational to global payments rather than alternative rails.“Within the next five years, stablecoins will be fully integrated into global payment systems,” she said, citing regulatory progress and adoption efforts by major payment networks including Visa and Mastercard.She added that stablecoins are increasingly viewed as efficient tools for cross-border settlement, liquidity management and real-time financial operations.Custody and AI convergence to drive next phaseLooking ahead, Long expects banks, financial institutions and crypto-native firms to increasingly custody digital assets directly as part of broader blockchain strategies.She also highlighted the convergence of artificial intelligence and blockchain as a key catalyst for the next growth phase.According to Long, smart contracts and stablecoins could enable treasuries to:Manage liquidity automaticallyExecute margin calls in real timeOptimize yield via onchain repo marketsPrivacy technology will be critical, she added, with zero-knowledge proofs allowing AI systems to assess credit risk and compliance without exposing sensitive data — a development she says could unlock adoption across heavily regulated markets.

data.title: Bermuda's Ambitious Move Towards a Fully On-Chain Economy with USDC
data.description: Bermuda is working towards becoming the first nation to operate a fully on-chain economy using USDC stablecoins to modernize its payment systems across government and businesses. According to NS3.AI, the initiative is currently in its early pilot stages, with a focus on reducing costs and modernizing infrastructure rather than achieving full adoption or mandating blockchain payments. While the project promises potential savings for merchants and sees growing institutional stablecoin settlements, such as those by Visa, the widespread consumer use and integration of these systems remain limited. This highlights a significant gap between the ambitious goals and the current reality.

data.title: SWIFT at Davos: Interoperable Tokenized Assets Can Accelerate Global Trade and Unlock Liquidity
data.description: At the World Economic Forum in Davos, global payments network SWIFT said that interoperable tokenized assets could significantly improve global trade efficiency, unlock new liquidity, and strengthen the connection between traditional finance and digital finance.SWIFT emphasized that enabling interoperability across tokenized asset systems would allow financial institutions to settle transactions faster, reduce friction in cross-border payments, and integrate blockchain-based assets into existing financial infrastructure.

data.description: Garrett Jin, an agent for "1011 Insider Whale," published an article on the X platform stating that Wintermute's viewpoint has been interpreted as "the traditional bull market cycle for cryptocurrencies has ended." This viewpoint is very commercial. Institutions have not ended the bull market, but rather changed it. As institutional asset management scale increases, asset prices tend to rise, while volatility decreases. BTC and ETH are undergoing a transition from a speculative mechanism to an allocation mechanism. Wintermute's actual view is that with the deepening of institutional participation, top crypto assets, led by BTC and ETH, are evolving from speculative tools into allocable assets. data.title: Challenges in Implementing Wallet Login in Crypto Platforms
data.description: DeFiLlama founder 0xngmi recently highlighted challenges in the implementation of 'Log in with Wallet' in the crypto industry. According to Odaily, while this method is frequently discussed, many platforms face practical obstacles. A significant issue is that users often forget the wallet address they used for registration. Additionally, the wallet login method makes it difficult to send timely critical notifications, such as API quota usage alerts, to users. Introducing email as a supplementary step can partially address these issues but undermines the original purpose of wallet login and increases user friction. Although wallet login remains valuable in applications requiring high security, such applications represent only a small portion of the overall market.

data.title: DeFiLlama founder: The actual implementation of wallet login methods on most crypto platforms faces far greater friction than expected.
data.description: DeFiLlama founder 0xngmi stated in an article on the X platform that although "login with wallet" is frequently discussed in the crypto industry, most crypto platforms face many practical obstacles in actual implementation. One of the biggest problems is that users often forget the wallet address they used to register. In addition, it is difficult to send key notifications (such as API quota usage reminders) to users in a timely manner under the wallet login mode. While introducing email as a supplementary step can partially solve the problem, it weakens the original intention of wallet login and increases friction in use. Although wallet login still has value in application scenarios with high security requirements, such applications only account for a small part of the overall market.

data.title: The founder of GMGN missed out on nearly $450,000 in profits after failing to take profits at the peak of his "memes" purchase.
data.description: GMGN founder Haze invested $869.50 in the meme coin "memes" during its initial public offering (IPO) phase when its market capitalization was $45,000. His unrealized profit initially reached $520,000. However, possibly influenced by a deleted tweet from He Yi, the market experienced a panic sell-off of the meme coin. Haze sold all his holdings during this period, at an average price of $3.83 million, realizing a profit of $72,900. Notably, the token subsequently rebounded, and as of press time, its market capitalization was $12.35 million. Odaily reminds users that the price of meme coins is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: Ethereum Co-Founder Predicts Decentralized Social Media Growth by 2026
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has predicted a revival of decentralized social media platforms by 2026. According to NS3.AI, Buterin emphasized the necessity for enhanced tools to foster a more robust society. He expressed concerns over existing crypto social projects, criticizing their dependence on speculative tokens that lead to financial bubbles and detract from content quality. Despite these issues, Buterin remains hopeful about the future development and influence of decentralized communication technologies.
